content.full_text:
RAy! MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
August11, 1997
Shirley L. Grindle
Orange County Campaign Finance Reform
5021 E. Glen Arran
Orange, California 92869
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-97-362
Dear Ms. Grindle:
This letter is a response to your request for advice about the Political Reform Act (the
“Act”).’
QUESTIONS
1. May a winning primary candidate who has primary campaign funds remaining (i.e.,
after paying primary campaign expenses) use those funds for the general election, or must they
be disposed ofpursuant to Section 89519?
2. If the remaining primary campaign funds may be used for general election expenses,
must the primary funds be transferred to another committee with its own bank account
established for the general election, or may the funds remain in the committee used for the
primary election and still be used for the general election?
RESPONSE
The Commission and the staff are developing regulations to address intra-candidate
transfers of campaign funds. We expect these upcoming regulations to address, among other
things, the transfer of campaign funds raised for one election in a given election cycle to another
election in the same election cycle (e.g., from primary to general).
Government Code sections 81000
- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 .1 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. A-97-362
Page 2
The Commission and the staff are still considering the legal and policy questions raised
by these issues. Because this work is not yet complete, we must respectfully decline to respond
to your questions at this time. We trust that the Commission will resolve these questions in the
next few months.
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
eneral Counsel
$lflJt•
John (vergeili
Staff’Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:JCV:jlw
Enclosure
